title: How to Play the Housing Market Slowdown
text: Prospective homebuyers and investors alike have been watching the U.S. housing market's meteoric rise over the past couple of years, with homebuyers hoping price increases would cool off soon and investors rejoicing in the opposite.

title: D.R. Horton: Exploit A Major Mid-Term Catalyst
text: Demographics drive returns in equities, especially when it comes to the housing sector. As millennials enter their prime productivity and homebuying years, the homebuilding industry presents a strong mid-term outlook.

title: These 2 Stocks Won't Grow Your Money Overnight, but Are No-Brainer Long-Term Buys
text: A bumpy housing market won't keep these stocks down forever.

title: Bruce Berkowitz Nabs Cheap Homebuilding Stocks in 2nd Quarter
text: Fairholme Capital Management recently disclosed its 13F portfolio updates for the second quarter of 2022, which ended on June 30.

title: 7 Stocks to Sell Before the 2022 Housing Market Crash
text: While no guarantees of a housing market crash exist, investors may need to start considering sector-related stocks to sell. Primarily, it's no longer amateur doom-and-gloom luminaries broadcasting bearishness.

title: The Housing Recession
text: Cracks in the nation's housing market continue to grow. Yesterday, the government reported that U.S. homebuilding sank to its lowest level in over a year, with housing starts plunging 9.6% as higher construction costs continue to weigh on builders.

title: D.R. Horton: Buyer Be Aware
text: At 7 times earnings (based on fiscal year 2021), D.R. Horton's stock is cheap. DHI's most recent earnings show net income increased 53%.

title: Homebuilder stocks drop as companies report 8th straight monthly drop in housing sentiment to the lowest reading since start of the pandemic
text: About 1-in-5 homebuilders reported reducing prices to boost sales or limit cancellations, according to the NAHB/Wells Fargo survey.

title: Is it safe to step back into the homebuilder stocks now?
text: New home sales have reached a bottom, which makes now a perfect time to go shopping in the construction space, says John Lovallo. He's a Senior Equity Research Analyst at UBS.

title: 7 Stocks to Sell After the Fed Rate Hike in July
text: As soaring inflation continues to impose a dark cloud over American families, the Federal Reserve seemingly had little choice but to lift the benchmark federal funds rate, which effectively raises borrowing costs. However, this action also sucks the incentivization out of growth-oriented businesses, meaning that some companies will invariably represent stocks to sell.

title: Time for a Breath of Sanity
text: After a drop of about 20% in the first six months of this year, investors feel nervous even though July has been good.
